name: leios_same_vote_different_signature
description: Voter 5 appears twice within the SAME MsgLeiosVotes batch for the same (slot, eb_hash) but with different signature bytes (aa0005 vs bb0005). From the spec perspective, a vote's identity is (voter_id, pipeline_slot, eb_hash) — the signature is a cryptographic proof of authenticity, not a distinguishing component. Two votes sharing the same identity tuple are duplicates regardless of whether their signatures differ. A conforming node must deduplicate on (voter_id, slot, eb_hash) and count voter 5's weight exactly once toward the EB1 tally. A node that uses the full tuple including signature as the dedup key would count voter 5 twice (once per signature), inflating the tally to 4 when the correct count is 3 (voters 5, 6, 7). This is an adversarial within-batch duplication attack distinct from the cross-batch case in leios_double_vote.
